qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: DNSSEC on-the-fly signing CPU exhaustion via random subdomain flood
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Does not match: this finding targets DNSSEC on-the-fly signing in plugin/dnssec via DO-bit random-subdomain cache misses, while the advisory targets DoQ worker pool backlog and blocking QUIC stream reads in core/dnsserver/server_quic.go.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described CoreDNS DoQ root cause: unbounded goroutines waiting on streamProcessPool tokens combined with blocking io.ReadFull() calls without per-stream read deadlines in core/dnsserver/server_quic.go.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2wpx-qpw2-g5h5 describes CoreDNS DNS-over-QUIC stream handling in core/dnsserver/server_quic.go, specifically streamProcessPool backlog behavior and blocking readDOQMessage() framing reads.
dnssec-cpu-exhaustion-dos targets plugin/dnssec/* signing behavior for DO-bit DNS queries and random-subdomain NSEC black-lie cache misses.
